file.content:
TARGET_DIR = target
REDACTED

install-stwo-run-and-prove:
	cargo +nightly-2025-07-14 install \
		--git ssh://git@github.com/m-kus/proving-utils.git \
		--rev $(PROVING_UTILS_REV) \
		stwo_run_and_prove --force

falcon-execute:
	rm -rf $(TARGET_DIR)/execute/falcon \
		&& cd packages/falcon \
		&& scarb execute --arguments-file tests/data/args_512_1.json --print-resource-usage --save-profiler-trace-data

falcon-args:
	python packages/falcon/scripts/generate_args.py --n 512 --num_signatures 1 > packages/falcon/tests/data/args_512_1.json
	python packages/falcon/scripts/generate_args.py --n 1024 --num_signatures 1 > packages/falcon/tests/data/args_1024_1.json

falcon-build:
	scarb --profile release build --package falcon

falcon-prove:
	stwo_run_and_prove \
		--program resources/simple_bootloader_compiled.json \
		--program_input packages/falcon/proving_task.json \
		--prover_params_json prover_params.json \
		--proofs_dir $(TARGET_DIR) \
		--proof-format cairo-serde \
		--verify

falcon-burn:
	scarb burn --package falcon \
		--arguments-file packages/falcon/tests/data/args_512_1.json \
		--output-file target/falcon.svg \
		--open-in-browser

regenerate-ntt:
	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate ntt --n 512

regenerate-intt:
	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate intt --n 512

regenerate-all:
	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate all --n 512

FALCON_RS_DIR = ../falcon-rs
WASM_OUT_DIR = apps/demo/public/wasm

wasm-build:
	cd $(FALCON_RS_DIR) && wasm-pack build --target web --features wasm --no-default-features
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs_bg.wasm $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs.js $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs.js
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs.d.ts $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs.d.ts
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs_bg.wasm.d.ts $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm.d.ts
	@echo "WASM built and copied to $(WASM_OUT_DIR)"
	@ls -lh $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm

DEMO_PORT = 3737

demo-serve:
	@if lsof -ti :$(DEMO_PORT) >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		echo "Port $(DEMO_PORT) already in use"; exit 1; \
	fi
	cd apps/demo && npx next dev --turbopack -H 0.0.0.0 -p $(DEMO_PORT)

demo-stop:
	@lsof -ti :$(DEMO_PORT) | xargs -r kill && echo "Demo stopped" || echo "Nothing on port $(DEMO_PORT)"

# --- E2E & Deployment ---

build-account:
	scarb build --package falcon_account

e2e-devnet:
	./bin/e2e-devnet.sh

declare-devnet: build-account
	./bin/declare.sh devnet

declare-sepolia: build-account
	./bin/declare.sh sepolia

deploy: wasm-build
	cd apps/demo && vercel --prod

1→/* tslint:disable */
     2→/* eslint-disable */
     3→
     4→/**
     5→ * Create a verification hint for Cairo on-chain verification.
     6→ *
     7→ * Computes `INTT(NTT(s1) * pk_ntt)` — the mul_hint that allows
     8→ * Cairo to verify NTT products with 2 forward NTTs and 0 INTTs.
     9→ *
    10→ * Parameters:
    11→ * - `s1`: 512 signature coefficients (each in [0, 12289))
    12→ * - `pk_ntt`: 512 public key NTT coefficients (each in [0, 12289))
    13→ *
    14→ * Returns: Vec of 512 hint coefficients (returned as Uint16Array to JS).
    15→ */
    16→export function create_verification_hint(s1: Int32Array, pk_ntt: Int32Array): Uint16Array;
    17→
    18→/**
    19→ * Generate a new Falcon-512 keypair.
    20→ *
    21→ * Returns a JavaScript object with `sk` (secret key bytes) and `vk` (verifying key bytes).
    22→ *
    23→ * Note: Key generation is slow (~1-2 minutes) due to NTRU complexity.
    24→ */
    25→export function keygen(seed: Uint8Array): any;
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * Pack a public key (512 Zq values) into 29 felt252 storage slots.
    29→ *
    30→ * Uses base-Q Horner encoding: 18 values per felt252 (9 per u128 half).
    31→ * This achieves a 17x calldata reduction (512 → 29 slots).
    32→ *
    33→ * Parameters:
    34→ * - `pk_ntt`: 512 public key NTT coefficients (each in [0, 12289))
    35→ *
    36→ * Returns: Array of 29 hex strings, each representing a felt252.
    37→ */
    38→export function pack_public_key_wasm(pk_ntt: Uint16Array): string[];
    39→
    40→/**
    41→ * Get the public key length in bytes.
    42→ */
    43→export function public_key_length(): number;
    44→
    45→/**
    46→ * Get the salt length in bytes.
    47→ */
    48→export function salt_length(): number;
    49→
    50→/**
    51→ * Sign a message with a secret key.
    52→ *
    53→ * Parameters:
    54→ * - `sk_bytes`: The secret key bytes (serialized as 4 × 896 = 3584 bytes via `SecretKey::to_bytes`)
    55→ * - `message`: The message to sign
    56→ * - `salt`: A 40-byte random salt (must be exactly `SALT_LEN` bytes)
    57→ *
    58→ * Returns a JavaScript object with:
    59→ * - `signature`: `Uint8Array` of 666 bytes (the full Falcon-512 signature)
    60→ * - `salt`: `Uint8Array` of 40 bytes (echo of the provided salt)
    61→ *
    62→ * # Notes
    63→ *
    64→ * The caller is responsible for generating a cryptographically random `salt` before
    65→ * calling this function. The same salt must not be reused for different messages.
    66→ */
    67→export function sign(sk_bytes: Uint8Array, message: Uint8Array, salt: Uint8Array): any;
    68→
    69→/**
    70→ * Sign a Starknet transaction hash using Falcon-512 with Poseidon hash-to-point.
    71→ *
    72→ * This produces a signature in the format expected by the Cairo FalconAccount contract:
    73→ * - 29 packed felt252 for s1 (signature polynomial)
    74→ * - 29 packed felt252 for mul_hint (verification hint)
    75→ * - 2 felt252 for the salt
    76→ * - 1 felt252 for the salt length
    77→ * Total: 61 felt252 elements.
    78→ *
    79→ * Parameters:
    80→ * - `sk_bytes`: Secret key bytes (serialized SecretKey)
    81→ * - `tx_hash`: Transaction hash as hex string (felt252)
    82→ * - `pk_ntt`: 512 public key NTT coefficients (each in [0, 12289))
    83→ *
    84→ * Returns: Array of 61 hex strings, each representing a felt252.
    85→ */
    86→export function sign_for_starknet(sk_bytes: Uint8Array, tx_hash: string, pk_ntt: Int32Array): string[];
    87→
    88→/**
    89→ * Verify a signature.
    90→ *
    91→ * Parameters:
    92→ * - `vk_bytes`: The verifying key (public key) bytes
    93→ * - `message`: The signed message
    94→ * - `signature`: The signature bytes
    95→ *
    96→ * Returns true if the signature is valid, false otherwise.
    97→ */
    98→export function verify(vk_bytes: Uint8Array, message: Uint8Array, signature: Uint8Array): boolean;
    99→
   100→export type InitInput = RequestInfo | URL | Response | BufferSource | WebAssembly.Module;
   101→
   102→export interface InitOutput {
   103→    readonly memory: WebAssembly.Memory;
   104→    readonly create_verification_hint: (a: number, b: number, c: number, d: number) => [number, number, number, number];
   105→    readonly keygen: (a: number, b: number) => [number, number, number];
   106→    readonly pack_public_key_wasm: (a: number, b: number) => [number, number, number, number];
   107→    readonly public_key_length: () => number;
   108→    readonly salt_length: () => number;
   109→    readonly sign: (a: number, b: number, c: number, d: number, e: number, f: number) => [number, number, number];
   110→    readonly sign_for_starknet: (a: number, b: number, c: number, d: number, e: number, f: number) => [number, number, number, number];
   111→    readonly verify: (a: number, b: number, c: number, d: number, e: number, f: number) => [number, number, number];
   112→    readonly __wbindgen_malloc: (a: number, b: number) => number;
   113→    readonly __wbindgen_realloc: (a: number, b: number, c: number, d: number) => number;
   114→    readonly __wbindgen_exn_store: (a: number) => void;
   115→    readonly __externref_table_alloc: () => number;
   116→    readonly __wbindgen_externrefs: WebAssembly.Table;
   117→    readonly __externref_table_dealloc: (a: number) => void;
   118→    readonly __wbindgen_free: (a: number, b: number, c: number) => void;
   119→    readonly __externref_drop_slice: (a: number, b: number) => void;
   120→    readonly __wbindgen_start: () => void;
   121→}
   122→
   123→export type SyncInitInput = BufferSource | WebAssembly.Module;
   124→
   125→/**
   126→ * Instantiates the given `module`, which can either be bytes or
   127→ * a precompiled `WebAssembly.Module`.
   128→ *
   129→ * @param {{ module: SyncInitInput }} module - Passing `SyncInitInput` directly is deprecated.
   130→ *
   131→ * @returns {InitOutput}
   132→ */
   133→export function initSync(module: { module: SyncInitInput } | SyncInitInput): InitOutput;
   134→
   135→/**
   136→ * If `module_or_path` is {RequestInfo} or {URL}, makes a request and
   137→ * for everything else, calls `WebAssembly.instantiate` directly.
   138→ *
   139→ * @param {{ module_or_path: InitInput | Promise<InitInput> }} module_or_path - Passing `InitInput` directly is deprecated.
   140→ *
   141→ * @returns {Promise<InitOutput>}
   142→ */
   143→export default function __wbg_init (module_or_path?: { module_or_path: InitInput | Promise<InitInput> } | InitInput | Promise<InitInput>): Promise<InitOutput>;
   144→

1→import { Context, Effect, Layer } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmLoadError } from "./errors"
     3→
     4→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     5→// WasmModule interface
     6→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
     7→
     8→export interface WasmModule {
     9→  readonly keygen: (seed: Uint8Array) => { sk: Uint8Array; vk: Uint8Array }
    10→  readonly sign: (
    11→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    12→    message: Uint8Array,
    13→    salt: Uint8Array,
    14→  ) => {
    15→    signature: Uint8Array
    16→    salt: Uint8Array
    17→  }
    18→  readonly verify: (
    19→    vkBytes: Uint8Array,
    20→    message: Uint8Array,
    21→    signature: Uint8Array,
    22→  ) => boolean
    23→  readonly create_verification_hint: (
    24→    s1: Int32Array,
    25→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    26→  ) => Uint16Array
    27→  readonly ntt_public_key: (pkTime: Int32Array) => Int32Array
    28→  readonly pack_public_key_wasm: (pkNtt: Uint16Array) => string[]
    29→  readonly public_key_length: () => number
    30→  readonly salt_length: () => number
    31→  readonly sign_for_starknet: (
    32→    skBytes: Uint8Array,
    33→    txHash: string,
    34→    pkNtt: Int32Array,
    35→  ) => string[]
    36→}
    37→
    38→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39→// Runtime type validation — no `as any` or `as unknown as WasmModule`
    40→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41→
    42→/**
    43→ * Runtime guard that checks every method in the WasmModule interface exists
    44→ * and is a function. Returns true only when all seven methods are present.
    45→ *
    46→ * This replaces the unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule` cast that was used in
    47→ * the original implementation.
    48→ *
    49→ * Validation scope: checks that each required key is present and is a
    50→ * function. Function arity and return types cannot be checked without
    51→ * invoking the function (which would have side effects), so only presence
    52→ * and kind are validated here.
    53→ *
    54→ * No type assertions are used: after the null/typeof guard, TypeScript
    55→ * narrows `mod` to `object`, which satisfies `Reflect.get`'s `target:
    56→ * object` parameter — property access is type-safe without any cast.
    57→ */
    58→export function isValidWasmModule(mod: unknown): mod is WasmModule {
    59→  if (mod === null || typeof mod !== "object") return false
    60→  // TypeScript narrows `mod` to `object` here — no type assertion needed.
    61→  // Reflect.get accepts `object` as its first argument directly.
    62→  const methods = [
    63→    "keygen",
    64→    "sign",
    65→    "verify",
    66→    "create_verification_hint",
    67→    "ntt_public_key",
    68→    "pack_public_key_wasm",
    69→    "public_key_length",
    70→    "salt_length",
    71→    "sign_for_starknet",
    72→  ] as const
    73→  return methods.every((method) => typeof Reflect.get(mod, method) === "function")
    74→}
    75→
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→// Context.Tag — WasmRuntime service tag
    78→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    79→
    80→export class WasmRuntime extends Context.Tag("WasmRuntime")<
    81→  WasmRuntime,
    82→  WasmModule
    83→>() {}
    84→
    85→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    86→// Internal load function — uses Effect.fn for automatic span tracing
    87→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88→
    89→const loadWasmModule = Effect.fn("WasmRuntime.load")(function* () {
    90→  const mod = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    91→    try: async () => {
    92→      // Dynamic import of the WASM JS glue generated by wasm-pack.
    93→      // The `/* webpackIgnore: true */` comment prevents bundlers from
    94→      // trying to resolve this path at build time.
    95→      const modulePath = "/wasm/falcon_rs.js"
    96→      const imported = await import(
    97→        /* webpackIgnore: true */
    98→        modulePath
    99→      )
   100→      // Run the WASM initializer (wasm-pack generates a default export
   101→      // that downloads and instantiates the .wasm binary).
   102→      await imported.default()
   103→      return imported
   104→    },
   105→    catch: (error) =>
   106→      new WasmLoadError({
   107→        message: `Failed to load WASM module: ${String(error)}`,
   108→      }),
   109→  })
   110→
   111→  // Runtime validation — replaces unsafe `as unknown as WasmModule`
   112→  if (!isValidWasmModule(mod)) {
   113→    return yield* Effect.fail(
   114→      new WasmLoadError({
   115→        message:
   116→          "WASM module is missing required methods. Expected: keygen, sign, verify, " +
   117→          "create_verification_hint, ntt_public_key, pack_public_key_wasm, public_key_length, salt_length, sign_for_starknet.",
   118→      }),
   119→    )
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  yield* Effect.log("WasmRuntime loaded successfully")
   123→  return mod
   124→})
   125→
   126→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   127→// WasmRuntimeLive — Layer that loads and validates the WASM module
   128→//
   129→// Effect's Layer memoization guarantees this effect runs at most once when
   130→// the Layer is provided to the application. There is no mutable global state
   131→// (`let cachedModule`); the singleton guarantee comes from Layer itself.
   132→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   133→
   134→export const WasmRuntimeLive = Layer.effect(WasmRuntime, loadWasmModule())
   135→

1→import { Effect } from "effect"
     2→import { WasmRuntime } from "./WasmRuntime"
     3→import {
     4→  KeygenError,
     5→  SigningError,
     6→  HintGenerationError,
     7→  PackingError,
     8→  VerificationError,
     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import type {
    11→  FalconKeypair,
    12→  FalconSignatureResult,
    13→  PackedPublicKey,
    14→} from "./types"
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// VK byte deserialization
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→/**
    21→ * Deserialize a Falcon-512 verifying key (896 bytes) into 512 time-domain
    22→ * coefficients stored as an Int32Array.
    23→ *
    24→ * NOTE: The VerifyingKey stores h in the TIME domain. Callers must NTT-transform
    25→ * the result (via wasm.ntt_public_key) before using it for verification or signing.
    26→ *
    27→ * Format (falcon-rs, PUBLIC_KEY_LEN = 896, no header):
    28→ *   - 512 coefficients x 14 bits each, packed LSB-first
    29→ *
    30→ * Algorithm: maintains a bit-buffer, loading bytes from the input one at a
    31→ * time and extracting 14-bit windows.
    32→ *
    33→ * Throws on invalid input (too few bytes, or any coefficient >= Q=12289).
    34→ */
    35→function parsePublicKeyTimeBytes(vkBytes: Uint8Array): Int32Array {
    36→  const Q = 12289
    37→  const N = 512
    38→  const BITS_PER_COEFF = 14
    39→  const DATA_BYTES = (N * BITS_PER_COEFF) / 8 // 896
    40→
    41→  if (vkBytes.length < DATA_BYTES) {
    42→    throw new Error(
    43→      `VK too short: expected at least ${DATA_BYTES} bytes, got ${vkBytes.length}`,
    44→    )
    45→  }
    46→
    47→  const coeffs = new Int32Array(N)
    48→  const mask = (1 << BITS_PER_COEFF) - 1 // 0x3FFF
    49→
    50→  let bitBuffer = 0
    51→  let bitsInBuffer = 0
    52→  let byteIndex = 0
    53→
    54→  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
    55→    while (bitsInBuffer < BITS_PER_COEFF) {
    56→      bitBuffer |= vkBytes[byteIndex++] << bitsInBuffer
    57→      bitsInBuffer += 8
    58→    }
    59→    const coeff = bitBuffer & mask
    60→    bitBuffer >>= BITS_PER_COEFF
    61→    bitsInBuffer -= BITS_PER_COEFF
    62→
    63→    if (coeff >= Q) {
    64→      throw new Error(
    65→        `Invalid VK coefficient ${coeff} at index ${i} (must be < ${Q})`,
    66→      )
    67→    }
    68→    coeffs[i] = coeff
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  return coeffs
    72→}
    73→
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→// FalconService
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→export class FalconService extends Effect.Service<FalconService>()(
    79→  "FalconService",
    80→  {
    81→    accessors: true,
    82→    // No pre-bundled dependencies: callers must provide WasmRuntime via Layer.
    83→    // This enables unit tests to inject a mock WasmRuntime without the live
    84→    // loader trying to fetch /wasm/falcon_rs.js at import time.
    85→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    86→      const wasm = yield* WasmRuntime
    87→
    88→      const generateKeypair = Effect.fn("Falcon.generateKeypair")(
    89→        function* (seed?: Uint8Array) {
    90→          const s =
    91→            seed ?? crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32))
    92→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
    93→            try: () => wasm.keygen(s),
    94→            catch: (error) =>
    95→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    96→          })
    97→          const publicKeyTime = yield* Effect.try({
    98→            try: () => parsePublicKeyTimeBytes(result.vk),
    99→            catch: (error) =>
   100→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
   101→          })
   102→          // Transform to NTT domain — required for Cairo verification and hint generation
   103→          const publicKeyNtt = yield* Effect.try({
   104→            try: () => new Int32Array(wasm.ntt_public_key(publicKeyTime)),
   105→            catch: (error) =>
   106→              new KeygenError({ message: `NTT transform failed: ${String(error)}` }),
   107→          })
   108→          return {
   109→            secretKey: result.sk,
   110→            verifyingKey: result.vk,
   111→            publicKeyNtt,
   112→          } satisfies FalconKeypair
   113→        },
   114→      )
   115→
   116→      const sign = Effect.fn("Falcon.sign")(
   117→        function* (
   118→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   119→          message: Uint8Array,
   120→        ) {
   121→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
   122→            try: () =>
   123→              wasm.sign(secretKey, message, crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(40))),
   124→            catch: (error) =>
   125→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   126→          })
   127→          return result satisfies FalconSignatureResult
   128→        },
   129→      )
   130→
   131→      const verify = Effect.fn("Falcon.verify")(
   132→        function* (
   133→          verifyingKey: Uint8Array,
   134→          message: Uint8Array,
   135→          signature: Uint8Array,
   136→        ) {
   137→          return yield* Effect.try({
   138→            try: () => wasm.verify(verifyingKey, message, signature),
   139→            catch: (error) =>
   140→              new VerificationError({
   141→                message: String(error),
   142→                step: "verify",
   143→              }),
   144→          })
   145→        },
   146→      )
   147→
   148→      const createHint = Effect.fn("Falcon.createHint")(
   149→        function* (
   150→          s1: Int32Array,
   151→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   152→        ) {
   153→          return yield* Effect.try({
   154→            try: () => wasm.create_verification_hint(s1, pkNtt),
   155→            catch: (error) =>
   156→              new HintGenerationError({ message: String(error) }),
   157→          })
   158→        },
   159→      )
   160→
   161→      const packPublicKey = Effect.fn("Falcon.packPublicKey")(
   162→        function* (
   163→          pkNtt: Uint16Array,
   164→        ) {
   165→          const slots = yield* Effect.try({
   166→            try: () => wasm.pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt),
   167→            catch: (error) =>
   168→              new PackingError({ message: String(error) }),
   169→          })
   170→          return { slots } satisfies PackedPublicKey
   171→        },
   172→      )
   173→
   174→      const deserializePublicKeyNtt = Effect.fn(
   175→        "Falcon.deserializePublicKeyNtt",
   176→      )(function* (vkBytes: Uint8Array) {
   177→        const pkTime = yield* Effect.try({
   178→          try: () => parsePublicKeyTimeBytes(vkBytes),
   179→          catch: (error) =>
   180→            new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
   181→        })
   182→        return yield* Effect.try({
   183→          try: () => new Int32Array(wasm.ntt_public_key(pkTime)),
   184→          catch: (error) =>
   185→            new KeygenError({ message: `NTT transform failed: ${String(error)}` }),
   186→        })
   187→      })
   188→
   189→      const signForStarknet = Effect.fn("Falcon.signForStarknet")(
   190→        function* (
   191→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   192→          txHash: string,
   193→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   194→        ) {
   195→          return yield* Effect.try({
   196→            try: () => wasm.sign_for_starknet(secretKey, txHash, pkNtt),
   197→            catch: (error) =>
   198→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   199→          })
   200→        },
   201→      )
   202→
   203→      return {
   204→        generateKeypair,
   205→        sign,
   206→        verify,
   207→        createHint,
   208→        packPublicKey,
   209→        deserializePublicKeyNtt,
   210→        signForStarknet,
   211→      }
   212→    }),
   213→  },
   214→) {}
   215→

     3→
     4→/**
     5→ * Create a verification hint for Cairo on-chain verification.
     6→ *
     7→ * Computes `INTT(NTT(s1) * pk_ntt)` — the mul_hint that allows
     8→ * Cairo to verify NTT products with 2 forward NTTs and 0 INTTs.
     9→ *
    10→ * Parameters:
    11→ * - `s1`: 512 signature coefficients (each in [0, 12289))
    12→ * - `pk_ntt`: 512 public key NTT coefficients (each in [0, 12289))
    13→ *
    14→ * Returns: Vec of 512 hint coefficients (returned as Uint16Array to JS).
    15→ */
    16→export function create_verification_hint(s1: Int32Array, pk_ntt: Int32Array): Uint16Array;
    17→
    18→/**
    19→ * Generate a new Falcon-512 keypair.
    20→ *
    21→ * Returns a JavaScript object with `sk` (secret key bytes) and `vk` (verifying key bytes).
    22→ *
    23→ * Note: Key generation is slow (~1-2 minutes) due to NTRU complexity.
    24→ */
    25→export function keygen(seed: Uint8Array): any;
    26→
    27→/**
    28→ * Pack a public key (512 Zq values) into 29 felt252 storage slots.
    29→ *
    30→ * Uses base-Q Horner encoding: 18 values per felt252 (9 per u128 half).
    31→ * This achieves a 17x calldata reduction (512 → 29 slots).
    32→ *
    33→ * Parameters:
    34→ * - `pk_ntt`: 512 public key NTT coefficients (each in [0, 12289))
    35→ *
    36→ * Returns: Array of 29 hex strings, each representing a felt252.
    37→ */
    38→export function pack_public_key_wasm(pk_ntt: Uint16Array): string[];

    43→export function public_key_length(): number;

     9→} from "./errors"
    10→import type {
    11→  FalconKeypair,
    12→  FalconSignatureResult,
    13→  PackedPublicKey,
    14→} from "./types"
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// VK byte deserialization
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→/**
    21→ * Deserialize a Falcon-512 verifying key (896 bytes) into 512 time-domain
    22→ * coefficients stored as an Int32Array.
    23→ *
    24→ * NOTE: The VerifyingKey stores h in the TIME domain. Callers must NTT-transform
    25→ * the result (via wasm.ntt_public_key) before using it for verification or signing.
    26→ *
    27→ * Format (falcon-rs, PUBLIC_KEY_LEN = 896, no header):
    28→ *   - 512 coefficients x 14 bits each, packed LSB-first
    29→ *
    30→ * Algorithm: maintains a bit-buffer, loading bytes from the input one at a
    31→ * time and extracting 14-bit windows.
    32→ *
    33→ * Throws on invalid input (too few bytes, or any coefficient >= Q=12289).
    34→ */
    35→function parsePublicKeyTimeBytes(vkBytes: Uint8Array): Int32Array {
    36→  const Q = 12289
    37→  const N = 512
    38→  const BITS_PER_COEFF = 14
    39→  const DATA_BYTES = (N * BITS_PER_COEFF) / 8 // 896
    40→
    41→  if (vkBytes.length < DATA_BYTES) {
    42→    throw new Error(
    43→      `VK too short: expected at least ${DATA_BYTES} bytes, got ${vkBytes.length}`,
    44→    )
    45→  }
    46→
    47→  const coeffs = new Int32Array(N)
    48→  const mask = (1 << BITS_PER_COEFF) - 1 // 0x3FFF
    49→
    50→  let bitBuffer = 0
    51→  let bitsInBuffer = 0
    52→  let byteIndex = 0
    53→
    54→  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
    55→    while (bitsInBuffer < BITS_PER_COEFF) {
    56→      bitBuffer |= vkBytes[byteIndex++] << bitsInBuffer
    57→      bitsInBuffer += 8
    58→    }
    59→    const coeff = bitBuffer & mask
    60→    bitBuffer >>= BITS_PER_COEFF
    61→    bitsInBuffer -= BITS_PER_COEFF
    62→
    63→    if (coeff >= Q) {
    64→      throw new Error(
    65→        `Invalid VK coefficient ${coeff} at index ${i} (must be < ${Q})`,
    66→      )
    67→    }
    68→    coeffs[i] = coeff
    69→  }
    70→
    71→  return coeffs
    72→}
    73→
    74→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    75→// FalconService
    76→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    77→
    78→export class FalconService extends Effect.Service<FalconService>()(
    79→  "FalconService",
    80→  {
    81→    accessors: true,
    82→    // No pre-bundled dependencies: callers must provide WasmRuntime via Layer.
    83→    // This enables unit tests to inject a mock WasmRuntime without the live
    84→    // loader trying to fetch /wasm/falcon_rs.js at import time.
    85→    effect: Effect.gen(function* () {
    86→      const wasm = yield* WasmRuntime
    87→
    88→      const generateKeypair = Effect.fn("Falcon.generateKeypair")(
    89→        function* (seed?: Uint8Array) {
    90→          const s =
    91→            seed ?? crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(32))
    92→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
    93→            try: () => wasm.keygen(s),
    94→            catch: (error) =>
    95→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
    96→          })
    97→          const publicKeyTime = yield* Effect.try({
    98→            try: () => parsePublicKeyTimeBytes(result.vk),
    99→            catch: (error) =>
   100→              new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
   101→          })
   102→          // Transform to NTT domain — required for Cairo verification and hint generation
   103→          const publicKeyNtt = yield* Effect.try({
   104→            try: () => new Int32Array(wasm.ntt_public_key(publicKeyTime)),
   105→            catch: (error) =>
   106→              new KeygenError({ message: `NTT transform failed: ${String(error)}` }),
   107→          })
   108→          return {
   109→            secretKey: result.sk,
   110→            verifyingKey: result.vk,
   111→            publicKeyNtt,
   112→          } satisfies FalconKeypair
   113→        },
   114→      )
   115→
   116→      const sign = Effect.fn("Falcon.sign")(
   117→        function* (
   118→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   119→          message: Uint8Array,
   120→        ) {
   121→          const result = yield* Effect.try({
   122→            try: () =>
   123→              wasm.sign(secretKey, message, crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(40))),
   124→            catch: (error) =>
   125→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   126→          })
   127→          return result satisfies FalconSignatureResult
   128→        },
   129→      )
   130→
   131→      const verify = Effect.fn("Falcon.verify")(
   132→        function* (
   133→          verifyingKey: Uint8Array,
   134→          message: Uint8Array,
   135→          signature: Uint8Array,
   136→        ) {
   137→          return yield* Effect.try({
   138→            try: () => wasm.verify(verifyingKey, message, signature),
   139→            catch: (error) =>
   140→              new VerificationError({
   141→                message: String(error),
   142→                step: "verify",
   143→              }),
   144→          })
   145→        },
   146→      )
   147→
   148→      const createHint = Effect.fn("Falcon.createHint")(
   149→        function* (
   150→          s1: Int32Array,
   151→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   152→        ) {
   153→          return yield* Effect.try({
   154→            try: () => wasm.create_verification_hint(s1, pkNtt),
   155→            catch: (error) =>
   156→              new HintGenerationError({ message: String(error) }),
   157→          })
   158→        },
   159→      )
   160→
   161→      const packPublicKey = Effect.fn("Falcon.packPublicKey")(
   162→        function* (
   163→          pkNtt: Uint16Array,
   164→        ) {
   165→          const slots = yield* Effect.try({
   166→            try: () => wasm.pack_public_key_wasm(pkNtt),
   167→            catch: (error) =>
   168→              new PackingError({ message: String(error) }),
   169→          })
   170→          return { slots } satisfies PackedPublicKey
   171→        },
   172→      )
   173→
   174→      const deserializePublicKeyNtt = Effect.fn(
   175→        "Falcon.deserializePublicKeyNtt",
   176→      )(function* (vkBytes: Uint8Array) {
   177→        const pkTime = yield* Effect.try({
   178→          try: () => parsePublicKeyTimeBytes(vkBytes),
   179→          catch: (error) =>
   180→            new KeygenError({ message: String(error) }),
   181→        })
   182→        return yield* Effect.try({
   183→          try: () => new Int32Array(wasm.ntt_public_key(pkTime)),
   184→          catch: (error) =>
   185→            new KeygenError({ message: `NTT transform failed: ${String(error)}` }),
   186→        })
   187→      })
   188→
   189→      const signForStarknet = Effect.fn("Falcon.signForStarknet")(
   190→        function* (
   191→          secretKey: Uint8Array,
   192→          txHash: string,
   193→          pkNtt: Int32Array,
   194→        ) {
   195→          return yield* Effect.try({
   196→            try: () => wasm.sign_for_starknet(secretKey, txHash, pkNtt),
   197→            catch: (error) =>
   198→              new SigningError({ message: String(error) }),
   199→          })
   200→        },
   201→      )
   202→
   203→      return {
   204→        generateKeypair,
   205→        sign,
   206→        verify,
   207→        createHint,
   208→        packPublicKey,
   209→        deserializePublicKeyNtt,
   210→        signForStarknet,
   211→      }
   212→    }),
   213→  },
   214→) {}
   215→

content.thinking:
The user is saying the issue is only about the build command - the WASM module is missing `ntt_public_key` because the Rust code doesn't export it. The `make deploy` runs `wasm-build` first, which rebuilds the WASM without `ntt_public_key`, then the app fails at runtime because the TypeScript code expects it.

So the fix is to either:
1. Add `ntt_public_key` to the Rust WASM bindings
2. Remove `ntt_public_key` from the TypeScript interface and implement the NTT in JS or restructure

Since the user says "there's nothing to fix, it's only about the build command" - wait, let me re-read. 

They say `make deploy` works but the WASM module bug exists. Then they say "fix the make deploy command." Then they clarify "there's nothing to fix it's only about the build command."

I think they mean: the `deploy` target depends on `wasm-build`, which rebuilds the WASM from source. But the current checked-in WASM files might have been manually patched or from an older version that DID have `ntt_public_key`. When `make deploy` runs, it rebuilds WASM (which doesn't have `ntt_public_key`), overwriting the working files, and then deploys a broken build.

So the real issue is that `make deploy` shouldn't rebuild the WASM - it should just deploy the already-built artifacts. The fix would be to remove the `wasm-build` dependency from the `deploy` target, or change it to only rebuild if the Rust source has actually changed. would be to either:
1. Remove the `wasm-build` dependency from `deploy` so it uses the committed WASM files
2. Or add `ntt_public_key` to the Rust code

Since the user says "there's nothing to fix it's only about the build command" - they probably want option 1: just remove the `wasm-build` dependency from `deploy`, so deploy uses whatever WASM files are already in the repo.

Wait, but the git status shows the WASM files are modified (unstaged). The committed versions might have `ntt_public_key` but the working tree versions (from a recent `wasm-build`) don't.

Actually, re-reading the user's message more carefully: "make deploy works but the wasm module bug". So the deploy succeeds (vercel uploads) but the deployed site has the bug because `wasm-build` rebuilt the files without the necessary key. The solution is straightforward—I need to remove `wasm-build` from the deploy target's dependencies so it uses the committed WASM files instead.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
TARGET_DIR = target
REDACTED

install-stwo-run-and-prove:
	cargo +nightly-2025-07-14 install \
		--git ssh://git@github.com/m-kus/proving-utils.git \
		--rev $(PROVING_UTILS_REV) \
		stwo_run_and_prove --force

falcon-execute:
	rm -rf $(TARGET_DIR)/execute/falcon \
		&& cd packages/falcon \
		&& scarb execute --arguments-file tests/data/args_512_1.json --print-resource-usage --save-profiler-trace-data

falcon-args:
	python packages/falcon/scripts/generate_args.py --n 512 --num_signatures 1 > packages/falcon/tests/data/args_512_1.json
	python packages/falcon/scripts/generate_args.py --n 1024 --num_signatures 1 > packages/falcon/tests/data/args_1024_1.json

falcon-build:
	scarb --profile release build --package falcon

falcon-prove:
	stwo_run_and_prove \
		--program resources/simple_bootloader_compiled.json \
		--program_input packages/falcon/proving_task.json \
		--prover_params_json prover_params.json \
		--proofs_dir $(TARGET_DIR) \
		--proof-format cairo-serde \
		--verify

falcon-burn:
	scarb burn --package falcon \
		--arguments-file packages/falcon/tests/data/args_512_1.json \
		--output-file target/falcon.svg \
		--open-in-browser

regenerate-ntt:
	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate ntt --n 512

regenerate-intt:
	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate intt --n 512

regenerate-all:
	python3 -m cairo_gen.circuits.regenerate all --n 512

FALCON_RS_DIR = ../falcon-rs
WASM_OUT_DIR = apps/demo/public/wasm

wasm-build:
	cd $(FALCON_RS_DIR) && wasm-pack build --target web --features wasm --no-default-features
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs_bg.wasm $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs.js $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs.js
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs.d.ts $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs.d.ts
	cp $(FALCON_RS_DIR)/pkg/falcon_rs_bg.wasm.d.ts $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm.d.ts
	@echo "WASM built and copied to $(WASM_OUT_DIR)"
	@ls -lh $(WASM_OUT_DIR)/falcon_rs_bg.wasm

DEMO_PORT = 3737

demo-serve:
	@if lsof -ti :$(DEMO_PORT) >/dev/null 2>&1; then \
		echo "Port $(DEMO_PORT) already in use"; exit 1; \
	fi
	cd apps/demo && npx next dev --turbopack -H 0.0.0.0 -p $(DEMO_PORT)

demo-stop:
	@lsof -ti :$(DEMO_PORT) | xargs -r kill && echo "Demo stopped" || echo "Nothing on port $(DEMO_PORT)"

build-account:
	scarb build --package falcon_account

e2e-devnet:

declare-devnet: build-account
	./bin/declare.sh devnet

declare-sepolia: build-account
	./bin/declare.sh sepolia

deploy: wasm-build
	cd apps/demo && vercel --prod
